# Ingredients

Grocery List

 \normalsize

- 1/3 cup chia seeds
- 1 tablespoon maple syrup
- 1 cup almond milk
- 1 cup of vanilla yogurt
- 2 tablespoons apricot jam
- 6 dried apricots
- 1/4 cup pumpkin seeds
- 1 pound 80/20 ground beef
- 4 brioche buns
- 4 slices of cheddar cheese
- pickle chips
- 1 tomato
- 1 red onion
- shredded lettuce
- 1 pound pasta (rigatoni or penne)
- 1 cup mayonnaise
- 1 can black olives
- 1 white onion
- 2 cans of baked beans
- 1 can of black beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 can of white kidney beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 can of red kidney beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 large yellow onion
- 1 pound of ground beef
- 1 pound of bacon
- 16 ounces of BBQ sauce
- 1 bag baby arugula (regular is fine, too)
- 1 pound pasta (e.g., bucatini or farfalle)
- 6-8 ounces of fresh or jarred pesto
- 1 lemon
- Grated parmesan cheese
- Pine nuts

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Recipe One

 \normalsize

![Burgers with pasta salad](website/images/Burgers.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 1 pound 80/20 ground beef
- 4 brioche buns
- 4 slices of cheddar cheese
- pickle chips
- 1 tomato
- 1 red onion
- shredded lettuce
- 1 pound pasta (rigatoni or penne)
- 1 cup mayonnaise
- 1 can black olives
- 1 white onion

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. Boil pasta according to package instructions. Place in a large bowl and cool to room temperature.
1. Dice onion and drain olives.
1. Combine pasta, onion, olives and mayonnaise. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
1. Refrigerate over night.
1. Thinly slice the tomato and red onion.
1. Mix ground beef with salt and pepper.
1. Create 4 evenly-sized patties. Put a dimple in the middle so it cooks to a consistent thickness.
1. Lightly toast the bun.
1. Build the burger by layering the burger, sliced tomato, onion, and pickles. Add ketchup, mustard, and mayonnaise to taste.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Recipe Two

 \normalsize

![BBQ Chili](website/images/BBQChili.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 2 cans of baked beans
- 1 can of black beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 can of white kidney beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 can of red kidney beans (drained and rinsed)
- 1 large yellow onion
- 1 pound of ground beef
- 1 pound of bacon
- 16 ounces of BBQ sauce

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. Dice the yellow onion.
1. Drain and rinse black, white, and red beans.
1. Crumble and cook beef in a frying pan.
1. Cook bacon and cut into bite size pieces.
1. Combine all ingredients in a crock pot. Cook at low heat for 6-7 hours. Stir occasionally.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Recipe Three


 \normalsize

![The Best Way to Eat Arugula (Serves 4)](website/images/ArugulaPasta.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 1 bag baby arugula (regular is fine, too)
- 1 pound pasta (e.g., bucatini or farfalle)
- 6-8 ounces of fresh or jarred pesto
- 1 lemon
- Grated parmesan cheese
- Pine nuts

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. Boil and cook pasta according to box, strain but reserve 1 cup pasta water.
1. In the stock pot used to cook the pasta, add two tablespoons of olive oil and whisk in pesto over medium heat.
1. Whisk in 1/2 cup pasta water, a little more if needed to create a light sauce.
1. Remove sauce from heat and toss cooked pasta in the sauce.
1. Stir arugula in and cover until wilted.
1. Add juice from half a lemon, add more to taste.
1. Serve with parmesan cheese and pine nuts.

 \normalsize

\newpage

# Breakfast Recipe

 \normalsize

![Chia Parfait](website/images/ChiaParfait.jpg){width=50%}

 \normalsize

**Ingredients**

 \normalsize

- 1/3 cup chia seeds
- 1 tablespoon maple syrup
- 1 cup almond milk
- 1 cup of vanilla yogurt
- 2 tablespoons apricot jam
- 6 dried apricots
- 1/4 cup pumpkin seeds

 \normalsize

**Instructions**

 \normalsize

1. Combine chia seeds, syrup, and milk with a pinch of salt in a 1-pint wide mouthed mason jar.
1. Whisk cover and refrigerate overnight.
1. Dice dried apricots.
1. Split between two 1-pint wide mouthed mason jars. Top with yogurt, apricot jam, dried apricots, and pumpkin seeds.

 \normalsize
